news_items.headline: Teaching AI models to say “I’m not sure”
news_items.summary:
# Teaching AI models to say “I’m not sure” | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology

Published: 2026-04-22T19:15:00+00:00

## Summary

Researchers from MIT's Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence Laboratory (CSAIL) have developed a method called RLCR (Reinforcement Learning with Calibration Rewards) that trains language models to produce calibrated confidence estimates alongside their answers. The method reduces calibration error by up to 90 percent while maintaining or improving accuracy on new tasks the model was trained on and on new ones it had never seen. The researchers

news_items.headline: SAGE Magnus Completes Phase 1 Pre-training: Architecture and Origins / News / SAGEA
news_items.summary:
SAGE Magnus Completes Phase 1 Pre-training: Architecture and Origins / News / SAGEA

Today, SAGE Magnus completes its Phase 1 pre-training. As we move into alignment and reinforcement learning, we want to address the public interest this project has generated and clarify what Magnus is, and how it was built.

## An MoE Architecture

Magnus is a Sparse Mixture of Experts model, not a dense one. This distinction matters. Training a large dense model from scratch was not computationally viable for our team. MoE allowed us to maintain high reasoning capacity while keeping active parameter counts t

news_items.summary:
# LLM Leaderboard: Best AI Models Ranked (April 2026) - DEV Community

Published: 2026-04-23T07:33:09+00:00

## Summary

There is no single best AI model in the world in April 2026, with Claude Opus 4.7 dominating coding benchmarks at 82% on SWE-bench Verified and ranking first on LM Arena with 1504 Elo rating. Three models tied at the top of the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index (score of 57), including Claude 4. 7, Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview, and GPT-5.4. news_items.headline: ChatGPT 5.5 just launched: features, performance, benchmarks, limits, and more
news_items.summary:
ChatGPT 5.5 just launched: features, performance, benchmarks, limits, and more

top of page

Search

GPT-5.5 is now live inside ChatGPT, and this release is being presented by OpenAI as a meaningful step forward in reasoning quality, agentic work, coding, research, and document-heavy execution rather than as a routine silent refresh.

Inside ChatGPT, the user-facing layer is GPT-5.5 Thinking, while higher-end plans also get GPT-5.5 Pro, which OpenAI describes as the stronger premium layer for harder questions and higher-accuracy work.

news_items.headline: DeepSeek V4评测结果_新浪新闻
news_items.summary:
DeepSeek V4评测结果_新浪新闻

高飞 26-04-24 13:29

微博认证：至顶科技创始人 AI博主

#DeepSeekV4发布# Arena AI评测总结：DeepSeek V4进步惊人，但和上个版本发布比较，不是同一种境况了今天的大事儿无疑就是DeepSeek发布了，当然再早一会儿，是GPT-5.5。还没来得及上手，但是看到Arena AI发了个评测视频《First impressions of DeepSeek V4 (open source)》，跟大家分享一下。先介绍Arena AI，它的前身为LMArena，是全球最大的AI模型众测排行平台，累计超过600万次用户盲投，产生基于Elo评分的公开排行榜。当然，我必须说，去年4月，Cohere Labs联合斯坦福、MIT、AI2等机构发表了68页论文《The Leaderboard Illusion》（后收录于NeurIPS 2025 Datasets and Benchmarks Track），指出它的测试有三个系统性问题：1、选择性公布：部分厂商（论文点名Meta、Google、OpenAI）被允许在正式发布前私下测试大量模型变体，只公布得分最高的那个。Meta在Llama 4发布前私下测试了27个变体，最终只公开了排名第二的那一个的成绩。2、采样不对等：闭源模型在盲测对战中被抽到的频率远高于开源模型，前

## Summary

Alibaba's Qwen Team has released Qwen3.6-27B, the first dense open-weight model in the Qwen 3.6 family, a 27-billion-parameter model available for coding agents. # Key Impacts of the 2026 National AI Legislative Framework on Healthcare

Published: 2026-04-25T01:53:11+00:00

## Summary

The 2026 National Artificial Intelligence (AI) legislative framework suggests a move towards federal AI legislation and potential preemption of state laws, creating a more uniform and strict compliance environment for healthcare and digital health companies. news_items.headline: Constitutional Collision: Questions to Assess the 2026 National AI Framework
news_items.summary:
# Constitutional Collision: Questions to Assess the 2026 National AI Framework | McDonnell Boehnen Hulbert & Berghoff LLP - JDSupra

Published: 2026-04-22T19:12:14+00:00

## Summary

The Trump administration's 2026 National Policy Framework for Artificial Intelligence (the Framework), released on March 20, 2026, aims to achieve a uniform, national policy for AI across the US. However, this will likely face constitutional challenges due to the Framework's distinction between AI "use" and "development". It allows states greater latitude to regulate AI use than AI development, which it defines as
